provenance_defaults.source: self-authored (multi-agent authored + adversarially verified; general clinical pharmacology knowledge, AU/AMH context; corroborate against AMH/TGA at review). NOT derived from LITFL notes.
provenance_defaults.authored_by: claude-fable-5 workflow (author+verify agents; draft — KL to attest)

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively antagonises the angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptor, blocking angiotensin II-mediated vasoconstriction and aldosterone release.
records.effect: Vasodilation, reduced aldosterone secretion and lowered blood pressure.

records.mechanism_of_action: Increases central GABAergic activity (inhibits GABA transaminase) and modulates voltage-gated sodium and T-type calcium channels, raising the seizure threshold.
records.target: GABA transaminase; voltage-gated sodium and T-type calcium channels
records.effect: Reduced neuronal excitability, anticonvulsant and mood-stabilising effect.

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks voltage-gated sodium channels in their inactivated state, stabilising neuronal membranes and limiting high-frequency repetitive firing and seizure spread.
records.effect: Membrane stabilisation and reduced propagation of seizure activity.

records.drug_class: Amide local anaesthetic; class Ib antiarrhythmic
records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks voltage-gated sodium channels on the neuronal membrane, preventing depolarisation and conduction of nerve impulses.
records.effect: Local anaesthesia; suppression of ventricular ectopy/arrhythmia.

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversibly inhibits cyclo-oxygenase (COX-1 and COX-2), reducing synthesis of prostaglandins involved in inflammation, pain and fever.
records.effect: Anti-inflammatory, analgesic and antipyretic effect.

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits bacterial dihydrofolate reductase, blocking conversion of dihydrofolate to tetrahydrofolate and impairing bacterial folate-dependent DNA/protein synthesis.
records.effect: Bacteriostatic inhibition of susceptible organisms.

records.mechanism_of_action: An osmotically active, minimally metabolised sugar alcohol that raises plasma and tubular fluid osmolality, drawing water from tissues into the intravascular space and reducing tubular water reabsorption.
records.target: No receptor; acts by osmotic gradient in plasma and renal tubule
records.effect: Osmotic diuresis and reduction of raised intracranial and intraocular pressure.

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-selectively stimulates alpha- and beta-adrenoceptors: alpha-1 mediated vasoconstriction, beta-1 mediated increased heart rate and contractility, and beta-2 mediated bronchodilation and mast-cell stabilisation.
records.effect: Vasoconstriction, increased cardiac output, bronchodilation and reversal of anaphylaxis.

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug hydrolysed to oseltamivir carboxylate, which inhibits influenza neuraminidase, preventing cleavage of sialic acid and blocking release of newly formed virions from infected cells.
records.effect: Reduced viral release and spread, shortening influenza illness.

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds reversibly to the 50S bacterial ribosomal subunit, blocking translocation and inhibiting protein synthesis; bacteriostatic (bactericidal at high concentrations against susceptible organisms).
records.target: Bacterial 50S ribosomal subunit (23S rRNA); also agonist at motilin receptors
records.effect: Inhibits growth of susceptible Gram-positive and atypical organisms; motilin agonism produces gastrointestinal prokinetic effect

records.mechanism_of_action: Sequential blockade of bacterial folate synthesis: sulfamethoxazole inhibits dihydropteroate synthase while trimethoprim inhibits dihydrofolate reductase, synergistically depleting tetrahydrofolate.
records.target: Bacterial dihydropteroate synthase (sulfamethoxazole) and dihydrofolate reductase (trimethoprim)
records.effect: Synergistic, usually bactericidal inhibition of nucleic acid and protein synthesis in susceptible organisms

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively antagonises acetylcholine at the nicotinic receptors of the postsynaptic motor end-plate, preventing depolarisation and muscle contraction.
records.target: Postsynaptic nicotinic acetylcholine receptor at the neuromuscular junction
records.effect: Skeletal muscle relaxation and flaccid paralysis (intermediate duration)

records.mechanism_of_action: Potent agonist at alpha-1 adrenergic receptors with modest beta-1 activity, producing vasoconstriction and increased systemic vascular resistance.
records.effect: Increased peripheral vascular resistance, arterial blood pressure and mean arterial pressure with minimal change in heart rate

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds penicillin-binding proteins (transpeptidases), inhibiting cross-linking of peptidoglycan in the bacterial cell wall and triggering autolysis; bactericidal.
records.effect: Bactericidal against susceptible Gram-positive cocci, Neisseria, spirochaetes and anaerobes

records.mechanism_of_action: Accumulates in the acidic parietal-cell canaliculus and irreversibly inhibits the gastric H+/K+-ATPase (proton pump), the final step of acid secretion.
records.effect: Marked and sustained suppression of basal and stimulated gastric acid secretion

records.mechanism_of_action: Directly stimulates alpha-1 adrenergic receptors and acts indirectly by displacing noradrenaline from sympathetic nerve terminals, producing vasoconstriction.
records.target: Alpha-1 adrenergic receptors (direct and indirect via noradrenaline release)
records.effect: Increased peripheral vascular resistance and blood pressure; may cause reflex bradycardia

records.drug_class: Fat-soluble vitamin / coagulation cofactor (phytomenadione, vitamin K1)
records.mechanism_of_action: Serves as the essential cofactor for hepatic gamma-glutamyl carboxylase, enabling gamma-carboxylation of clotting factors II, VII, IX and X and proteins C and S into functional forms.
records.effect: Restores synthesis of functional vitamin K-dependent clotting factors, reversing vitamin K-dependent coagulopathy (including warfarin effect)

records.mechanism_of_action: Positive allosteric modulator of the GABA-A receptor; binds the benzodiazepine site to increase the frequency of chloride-channel opening in response to GABA, enhancing inhibitory neurotransmission.
records.effect: CNS depression producing anxiolytic, sedative, anticonvulsant and centrally-mediated skeletal muscle relaxant effects

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-selective phosphodiesterase inhibition raising intracellular cAMP/cGMP, plus adenosine receptor antagonism; produces airway smooth muscle relaxation and respiratory/CNS stimulation.
records.effect: Bronchodilation, respiratory stimulation, mild diuresis

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversible inhibition of carbonic anhydrase, reducing bicarbonate reabsorption and the formation of aqueous humour and CSF; induces bicarbonate diuresis and metabolic acidosis.
records.effect: Reduced intraocular pressure, alkaline diuresis, correction of respiratory alkalosis at altitude

records.mechanism_of_action: Selective agonist at 5-HT1B and 5-HT1D receptors, causing constriction of dilated cranial vessels and inhibition of trigeminal nerve pro-inflammatory neuropeptide release.
records.effect: Abortive relief of acute migraine and cluster headache

records.mechanism_of_action: Guanosine analogue selectively phosphorylated by viral thymidine kinase to aciclovir triphosphate, which inhibits viral DNA polymerase and terminates viral DNA chain elongation.
records.effect: Inhibition of herpes simplex and varicella-zoster virus replication

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive antagonist at the benzodiazepine binding site of the GABA-A receptor, blocking benzodiazepine-mediated potentiation of GABA and reversing sedation.
records.effect: Reversal of benzodiazepine sedation and respiratory depression

records.mechanism_of_action: Plasminogen activators that convert plasminogen to plasmin, which enzymatically degrades fibrin and dissolves formed thrombus.
records.effect: Thrombus lysis and restoration of vascular patency/reperfusion

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds penicillin-binding proteins, inhibiting the transpeptidation cross-linking of peptidoglycan in the bacterial cell wall; the resulting cell wall instability is bactericidal.
records.effect: Broad-spectrum bactericidal activity, notably against Gram-negative organisms

records.mechanism_of_action: Activates A1 adenosine receptors in the AV node, opening inward-rectifier potassium channels and inhibiting calcium influx, transiently slowing or blocking AV nodal conduction.
records.effect: Transient AV nodal block, terminating AV-nodal-dependent re-entrant SVT

records.mechanism_of_action: Central dopamine D2 receptor antagonism at the chemoreceptor trigger zone gives antiemesis; peripheral D2 antagonism with 5-HT4 agonism enhances gastric and upper GI motility.
records.effect: Antiemetic effect and accelerated gastric emptying/prokinesis

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits bacterial DNA-dependent RNA polymerase by binding its beta-subunit (rpoB), blocking initiation of RNA transcription.
records.effect: Bactericidal; suppresses bacterial RNA synthesis

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits bacterial DNA gyrase (topoisomerase II) and topoisomerase IV, preventing supercoiling and separation of replicating DNA.
records.target: Bacterial DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV
records.effect: Bactericidal; blocks DNA replication

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the bacterial 50S ribosomal subunit and inhibits peptidyl transferase, blocking peptide-bond formation and protein synthesis.
records.target: Bacterial 50S ribosomal subunit (23S rRNA peptidyl transferase centre)
records.effect: Predominantly bacteriostatic

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversibly blocks voltage-gated sodium channels on the neuronal membrane, preventing depolarisation and conduction of nerve impulses.
records.effect: Reversible local/regional anaesthesia and conduction block

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits angiotensin-converting enzyme, reducing conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II and decreasing aldosterone; also reduces bradykinin degradation.
records.effect: Vasodilation, lowered blood pressure and cardiac afterload

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds penicillin-binding proteins and inhibits the transpeptidation step of peptidoglycan cross-linking, disrupting bacterial cell wall synthesis.
records.effect: Bactericidal; cell wall synthesis inhibition

records.mechanism_of_action: Positive allosteric modulator at the benzodiazepine site of the GABA-A receptor, increasing the frequency of chloride-channel opening and enhancing GABA-mediated inhibition.
records.effect: Sedation, anxiolysis, anterograde amnesia, anticonvulsant and muscle-relaxant effects

records.mechanism_of_action: Activates hepatic Gs-coupled glucagon receptors, raising intracellular cAMP to stimulate glycogenolysis and gluconeogenesis; also relaxes GI smooth muscle and exerts positive inotropic/chronotropic effects.
records.effect: Raises blood glucose; relaxes gastrointestinal smooth muscle

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively antagonises nicotinic acetylcholine receptors at the postsynaptic motor end-plate, preventing acetylcholine-induced depolarisation.
records.effect: Skeletal muscle relaxation/paralysis

records.mechanism_of_action: CNS depressant that potentiates inhibitory GABA-A receptor activity and inhibits excitatory NMDA glutamate receptors; as an antidote it competitively saturates alcohol dehydrogenase, blocking formation of toxic metabolites from methanol and ethylene glycol.
records.drug_class: CNS depressant; alcohol-dehydrogenase substrate antidote; antiseptic/sclerosant
records.target: GABA-A receptor, NMDA receptor, alcohol dehydrogenase
records.effect: Dose-dependent CNS depression; competitive inhibition of toxic-alcohol metabolism; local antisepsis/tissue ablation

records.mechanism_of_action: Recombinant human erythropoietin binds the erythropoietin receptor on marrow erythroid progenitors, promoting their proliferation, differentiation and survival, thereby increasing red cell production.
records.target: Erythropoietin receptor on erythroid progenitor cells
records.effect: Increased erythropoiesis and rise in haemoglobin

records.mechanism_of_action: Central analgesic/antipyretic acting largely through inhibition of prostaglandin synthesis in the CNS, with contributions from serotonergic descending pathways and the active metabolite AM404 (endocannabinoid/TRPV1 modulation); minimal peripheral anti-inflammatory effect.
records.target: Cyclooxygenase (central); serotonergic and endocannabinoid/TRPV1 pathways via metabolite
records.effect: Analgesia and antipyresis

records.mechanism_of_action: Class Ic antiarrhythmic that potently blocks the cardiac fast voltage-gated sodium channel, markedly slowing phase 0 depolarisation and conduction velocity with minimal effect on action potential duration.
records.effect: Slowed cardiac conduction and membrane stabilisation

records.mechanism_of_action: Depolarising neuromuscular blocker that acts as an agonist at postsynaptic nicotinic acetylcholine receptors of the neuromuscular junction, producing sustained depolarisation that inactivates sodium channels and prevents further muscle contraction.
records.target: Nicotinic acetylcholine receptor at the neuromuscular junction
records.effect: Rapid-onset, short-duration flaccid skeletal muscle paralysis

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds tubulin and inhibits microtubule polymerisation, impairing neutrophil chemotaxis, adhesion and migration and suppressing NLRP3 inflammasome activation, thereby dampening the acute inflammatory response to urate crystals.
records.effect: Reduced neutrophil-mediated inflammation

records.mechanism_of_action: First-generation cephalosporin beta-lactam that binds penicillin-binding proteins and inhibits transpeptidation in bacterial cell-wall peptidoglycan synthesis, producing a bactericidal effect.
records.effect: Bactericidal (time-dependent killing)

records.mechanism_of_action: Nitric oxide donor that releases NO to activate soluble guanylate cyclase, raising cGMP and relaxing both arterial and venous vascular smooth muscle, producing rapid balanced reduction in preload and afterload.
records.target: Vascular smooth muscle NO–cGMP pathway (soluble guanylate cyclase)
records.effect: Immediate, titratable reduction in blood pressure and cardiac loading

records.mechanism_of_action: Low-potency phenothiazine antipsychotic that antagonises dopamine D2 receptors (mesolimbic and other pathways), with additional blockade of histamine H1, muscarinic, alpha-1 adrenergic and 5-HT2 receptors accounting for its sedative, hypotensive and anticholinergic profile.
records.target: Dopamine D2 receptor (also H1, muscarinic, alpha-1 adrenergic, 5-HT2)
records.effect: Antipsychotic, sedative and antiemetic effects

records.mechanism_of_action: Methanol itself is a CNS depressant, but toxicity is metabolite-driven: hepatic alcohol dehydrogenase oxidises it to formaldehyde, then aldehyde dehydrogenase to formic acid/formate. Formate inhibits mitochondrial cytochrome c oxidase (complex IV), producing histotoxic hypoxia, high anion-gap metabolic acidosis, and optic nerve/retinal injury.
records.target: alcohol dehydrogenase (metabolic activation); formate inhibits cytochrome c oxidase (mitochondrial complex IV)
records.effect: CNS depression, high anion-gap metabolic acidosis, and visual toxicity (blindness)

records.mechanism_of_action: Reversibly blocks voltage-gated sodium channels on the internal surface of the neuronal membrane, preventing sodium influx, membrane depolarisation and propagation of the nerve action potential, thereby blocking conduction.
records.target: voltage-gated sodium channels (Nav) on peripheral nerve axons
records.effect: reversible local/regional anaesthesia (loss of sensation)

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the barbiturate site of the GABA-A receptor and potentiates GABAergic inhibition by prolonging chloride-channel opening (and at higher concentrations directly activating the channel), producing rapid CNS depression, hypnosis and anaesthesia; also lowers cerebral metabolic rate and intracranial pressure.
records.target: GABA-A receptor (barbiturate binding site / chloride channel)
records.effect: rapid induction of anaesthesia and hypnosis; anticonvulsant; reduces cerebral metabolic demand

records.mechanism_of_action: Deliver water, electrolytes (and sometimes glucose or colloid) directly into the vascular compartment to expand intravascular and extracellular fluid volume and correct water/electrolyte deficits; distribution between compartments is governed by the fluid's tonicity and composition.
records.target: intravascular / extracellular fluid compartment (volume and electrolyte homeostasis)
records.effect: volume expansion, restoration of tissue perfusion, and correction of fluid/electrolyte deficits

records.mechanism_of_action: Positive allosteric modulator at the benzodiazepine site of the GABA-A receptor, increasing the frequency of chloride-channel opening in response to GABA and enhancing inhibitory neurotransmission, producing anticonvulsant, anxiolytic, sedative and muscle-relaxant effects.
records.effect: anticonvulsant, anxiolytic, sedative and muscle-relaxant

records.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits the sodium-chloride cotransporter (NCC) in the luminal membrane of the distal convoluted tubule, reducing sodium and chloride reabsorption and increasing their urinary excretion with accompanying water (natriuresis/diuresis); chronic antihypertensive effect involves a fall in peripheral vascular resistance.
records.target: sodium-chloride cotransporter (NCC / SLC12A3) in the distal convoluted tubule
records.effect: diuresis, natriuresis and lowering of blood pressure

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks central muscarinic acetylcholine receptors, helping restore the dopamine-acetylcholine balance in the striatum that is disturbed in parkinsonism and by dopamine-blocking drugs; it also has antihistaminic activity and some inhibition of presynaptic dopamine reuptake.
records.effect: reduces tremor, rigidity and drug-induced extrapyramidal symptoms

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitively antagonises acetylcholine at postsynaptic nicotinic acetylcholine receptors on the motor end-plate of the neuromuscular junction, preventing end-plate depolarisation and producing skeletal muscle relaxation without initial fasciculation.
records.effect: rapid-onset skeletal muscle relaxation/paralysis

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively antagonises 5-HT3 receptors on peripheral vagal afferent nerves in the gut and centrally in the chemoreceptor trigger zone (area postrema), blocking serotonin-triggered activation of the vomiting reflex.
records.target: 5-HT3 serotonin receptors (peripheral vagal afferents and area postrema)
records.effect: prevention and treatment of nausea and vomiting

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive antagonist at opioid receptors; displaces opioid agonists from mu-receptors, rapidly reversing opioid effects.
records.effect: Reverses opioid-induced respiratory depression, sedation and analgesia

records.mechanism_of_action: Potentiates GABA-A receptor chloride currents, producing rapid dose-dependent CNS depression.
records.effect: Sedation, hypnosis and general anaesthesia with rapid onset and offset

records.mechanism_of_action: Directly and reversibly inhibits factor Xa, reducing thrombin generation and clot formation.
records.drug_class: Direct oral anticoagulant (direct factor Xa inhibitor)
records.effect: Anticoagulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Supplies calcium cation, an essential cofactor for neuromuscular transmission, myocardial and skeletal muscle contraction, and the coagulation cascade; stabilises the cardiac membrane potential.
records.target: Physiological calcium-dependent processes (not a discrete receptor)
records.effect: Corrects hypocalcaemia and stabilises cardiac membranes in hyperkalaemia

records.mechanism_of_action: Under anaerobic conditions the nitro group is reduced to reactive intermediates that damage microbial DNA, causing strand breakage and cell death.
records.effect: Bactericidal against anaerobic bacteria; antiprotozoal activity

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-selective beta-adrenoceptor blockade combined with class III blockade of the delayed rectifier potassium current (IKr), prolonging cardiac repolarisation.
records.target: Beta-1/beta-2 adrenoceptors and the rapid delayed-rectifier potassium channel (IKr/hERG)
records.effect: Slows heart rate and prolongs the action potential/QT interval; suppresses arrhythmias

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive non-selective antagonism of beta-1 and beta-2 adrenoceptors, reducing sympathetic drive to the heart and other tissues.
records.effect: Reduces heart rate, contractility and blood pressure; blunts tremor and somatic anxiety symptoms

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds penicillin-binding proteins and inhibits bacterial cell-wall peptidoglycan cross-linking, producing cell lysis.
records.effect: Bactericidal; broad-spectrum including Pseudomonas aeruginosa

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the 50S ribosomal subunit (23S rRNA) and blocks peptide translocation, inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis.
records.effect: Bacteriostatic (bactericidal at high concentration); extensive intracellular/tissue accumulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks voltage-gated sodium channels in their inactivated state, stabilising neuronal membranes and limiting high-frequency repetitive firing.
records.effect: Anticonvulsant, mood-stabilising, and analgesic in neuropathic pain

records.mechanism_of_action: Dopamine precursor that crosses the blood-brain barrier and is decarboxylated by aromatic L-amino-acid (DOPA) decarboxylase to dopamine, replenishing depleted striatal dopamine.
records.effect: Reduces bradykinesia, rigidity and tremor of Parkinson disease

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the 30S ribosomal subunit and blocks aminoacyl-tRNA attachment to the mRNA-ribosome complex, inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis.
records.effect: Bacteriostatic, broad-spectrum including atypical and intracellular organisms

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively inhibits the presynaptic serotonin reuptake transporter, increasing synaptic serotonin availability.
records.effect: Antidepressant, anti-obsessional and anxiolytic effects over weeks

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds antithrombin III and greatly accelerates its inactivation of thrombin (factor IIa) and factor Xa.
records.effect: Rapid anticoagulation; inhibits fibrin formation and clot propagation

records.mechanism_of_action: Blocks L-type voltage-gated calcium channels in cardiac and vascular smooth muscle, slowing AV-nodal conduction and reducing contractility and vascular tone.
records.effect: Negative chronotropy/dromotropy/inotropy, coronary and peripheral vasodilation

records.mechanism_of_action: Physiological cofactor and membrane stabiliser that antagonises calcium at channels and the NMDA receptor, reducing neuromuscular and myocardial excitability and relaxing smooth muscle.
records.target: Calcium channels, NMDA receptor, enzyme cofactor sites
records.effect: Anticonvulsant, antiarrhythmic, smooth-muscle relaxant and electrolyte repletion

records.mechanism_of_action: Block presynaptic reuptake of noradrenaline and serotonin by inhibiting their neuronal transporters, increasing synaptic monoamine availability; additionally antagonise muscarinic, histamine H1 and alpha-1 adrenergic receptors, which accounts for much of their adverse-effect profile.
records.target: Noradrenaline (NET) and serotonin (SERT) transporters; also muscarinic ACh, histamine H1 and alpha-1 adrenergic receptors
records.effect: Antidepressant, analgesic (neuropathic), sedating and anticholinergic effects

records.mechanism_of_action: Precise mechanism uncertain; modulates neuronal signalling by inhibiting inositol monophosphatase (depleting the phosphatidylinositol second-messenger cycle) and glycogen synthase kinase-3 (GSK-3), with downstream effects on neurotransmission and neuroprotection.
records.effect: Mood stabilisation; anti-manic and prophylactic effect in bipolar disorder

records.mechanism_of_action: Potent agonist at mu-opioid receptors in the CNS and periphery, inhibiting ascending nociceptive transmission and altering pain perception; highly lipophilic with rapid onset.
records.effect: Potent analgesia, sedation, respiratory depression, euphoria

records.mechanism_of_action: Synthetic octapeptide analogue of somatostatin; agonist at somatostatin receptors (predominantly SSTR2 and SSTR5), suppressing secretion of growth hormone, glucagon, insulin and gastroenteropancreatic hormones and reducing splanchnic blood flow and GI secretions.
records.effect: Inhibition of GH and GI hormone secretion; reduced splanchnic/portal blood flow and gut secretion

records.mechanism_of_action: Non-competitive antagonist at the NMDA subtype of glutamate receptor, producing dissociative anaesthesia and analgesia; also interacts with opioid, monoaminergic and muscarinic receptors and HCN channels.
records.effect: Dissociative anaesthesia, analgesia, bronchodilation, sympathomimetic cardiovascular stimulation

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the transmembrane insulin receptor tyrosine kinase, triggering signalling that promotes GLUT4-mediated glucose uptake into muscle and adipose tissue, stimulates glycogenesis, lipogenesis and protein synthesis, and suppresses hepatic gluconeogenesis and lipolysis.
records.effect: Lowers blood glucose; anabolic effects on carbohydrate, lipid and protein metabolism

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds the SUR1 subunit of ATP-sensitive potassium channels on pancreatic beta cells, closing them; the resulting membrane depolarisation opens voltage-gated calcium channels, and calcium influx triggers insulin secretion (an insulin secretagogue).
records.target: SUR1 subunit of the beta-cell ATP-sensitive potassium (K-ATP) channel
records.effect: Stimulates endogenous insulin secretion, lowering blood glucose

records.mechanism_of_action: Competitive reversible antagonist at muscarinic acetylcholine receptors, blocking parasympathetic (and some CNS) cholinergic tone.
records.effect: Increased heart rate, reduced secretions, bronchodilation, mydriasis and cycloplegia, smooth-muscle antispasmodic action

records.mechanism_of_action: Semisynthetic opioid agonist acting principally at mu-opioid receptors (with some kappa activity) in the CNS to inhibit nociceptive transmission and alter pain perception.
records.effect: Analgesia, sedation, euphoria, respiratory depression

records.mechanism_of_action: Diffuse into cells and bind the cytoplasmic glucocorticoid receptor; the activated complex translocates to the nucleus and alters gene transcription (transactivation of anti-inflammatory genes, transrepression of pro-inflammatory genes), suppressing cytokine production, phospholipase A2/COX-2 activity and leucocyte function.
records.effect: Anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive; also mineralocorticoid (salt/water retention) and metabolic effects varying by agent.

records.mechanism_of_action: Undergoes enzymatic denitration in vascular smooth muscle to release nitric oxide, which activates soluble guanylate cyclase, raising cGMP and causing smooth muscle relaxation; predominant venodilation reduces preload, with coronary vasodilation and some arterial effect at higher doses.
records.target: Soluble guanylate cyclase (via nitric oxide donation)
records.effect: Venodilation with reduced cardiac preload, coronary vasodilation and reduced myocardial oxygen demand.

records.mechanism_of_action: Antagonises dopamine D2 and serotonin 5-HT2A receptors, with additional antagonism at muscarinic, histamine H1 and alpha-1 adrenergic receptors; balanced 5-HT2A/D2 blockade underlies antipsychotic efficacy with lower extrapyramidal liability.
records.effect: Antipsychotic and mood-stabilising; sedating and antiemetic.

records.mechanism_of_action: Selectively and competitively blocks post-synaptic alpha-1 adrenergic receptors, inhibiting noradrenaline-mediated vasoconstriction of arterioles and veins and relaxing bladder-neck/prostatic smooth muscle.
records.effect: Reduced peripheral vascular resistance and blood pressure; reduced bladder outlet resistance.

records.mechanism_of_action: Binds with high affinity to the terminal D-alanyl-D-alanine of peptidoglycan precursors, sterically inhibiting transglycosylation and transpeptidation and thereby blocking bacterial cell-wall synthesis in Gram-positive organisms.
records.effect: Time-dependent bactericidal activity against Gram-positive bacteria including MRSA.

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug whose active thiol metabolite irreversibly binds the platelet P2Y12 ADP receptor, preventing ADP-induced activation of the glycoprotein IIb/IIIa complex and inhibiting platelet aggregation for the life of the platelet.
records.effect: Irreversible inhibition of ADP-mediated platelet aggregation.

records.mechanism_of_action: Provides cysteine to replenish hepatic glutathione and supplies sulfhydryl groups that detoxify the reactive paracetamol metabolite NAPQI; as a mucolytic it cleaves disulfide bonds in mucus glycoproteins, lowering viscosity.
records.target: Glutathione repletion / NAPQI conjugation; mucus disulfide bonds
records.effect: Hepatoprotection in paracetamol poisoning; reduced mucus viscosity.

records.mechanism_of_action: High-potency, largely selective antagonist of dopamine D2 receptors, particularly in mesolimbic pathways, reducing dopaminergic neurotransmission.
records.effect: Antipsychotic and antiemetic; calming in acute agitation, with notable extrapyramidal liability.

records.mechanism_of_action: Prodrug hydrolysed to ramiprilat, which inhibits ACE, reducing conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II (lowering vasoconstriction and aldosterone) and decreasing bradykinin degradation.
records.effect: Vasodilation, reduced afterload and blood pressure, natriuresis and renoprotection.
